disposal_conditions.dropoff: Bring radiators and space heaters to city transfer stations . Fees apply. Check the rates page.
disposal_conditions.special: Submit a Special Item Pickup online or call SPU Customer Service at (206) 684-3000 to schedule a bulky item pickup.
disposal_conditions.donate: Resell or give radiators or heaters away using sites like Craigslist , Freecycle , and Buy Nothing .

disposal_conditions.garbage: Empty and dry container go in the garbage: • Remove the lid and let fumes evaporate outside, away from children and animals. • Separate the lid from the dried finish and put both in your garbage.
disposal_conditions.hazardous: Bring wood finishes like polyurethane and stains to a household hazardous waste facility • Limit 50-gallons per trip. • Wood finishes are not allowed in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.dropoff: Recycle your paint products for free at a PaintCare drop-off site. Accepted products include latex paint, oil-based paint, stain, varnish, and more. Visit the PaintCare website to find a drop-off location near you and to view a list of accepted products.

disposal_conditions.garbage: All cotton swabs and cotton balls go in the garbage.

items.item_name: Medical Equipment and Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)

disposal_conditions.recycling: • Damage equipment made of aluminum (like wheelchair frames or crutches) can be recycled if cloth/rubber pieces are removed.
disposal_conditions.garbage: • All face masks, gloves, and other personal protective equipment (PPE) go in the garbage. • Latex gloves used in a non-medical context go in the garbage. • It does not matter what material the mask is made of. • Cut loops on masks before disposal. • Durable medical equipment that is damaged or unusable. • Glucose monitoring sensors and related equipment • Contact lenses and individual packaging
disposal_conditions.donate: • Durable medical equipment in good condition (like crutches or wheelchairs) can be donated to local thrift stores. • Resell or give away equipment using sites like Craigslist, Freecycle, and Buy Nothing. • You can also research where to donate durable medical equipment in good condition, there are many organizations that accept these donations. • If you have wooden crutches, consider upcycling them into a craft project--look online for inspiration!

disposal_conditions.garbage: Cellophane wrap and packaging material goes in the garbage. Cellophane is not approved for composting.

Plastic coolers are garbage. Place the cooler in the garbage if the garbage lid will close. Place next to garbage bin as extra garbage if the lid will not close. There is a small fee for extra garbage.
disposal_conditions.donate: If in usable condition, consider bringing your cooler to your local thrift store or giving it away to a friend or family member. You can also sell it on Craigslist or Facebook marketplace!

disposal_conditions.garbage: Plastic yard signs are garbage. They are not recyclable.
disposal_conditions.donate: Plastic yard signs are garbage. They are not recyclable. Check with the organization that provided the sign to see if they will take it back to repurpose or reuse the sign.

disposal_conditions.compost: Food packaging explicitly labelled #7 PLA is compostable in Seattle’s curbside collection system. It goes in the Food and Yard Waste container. • See Compostable Food Service Items for greater detail on compostable food service materials. • Go to SPU’s Food Service Packaging Requirements page for information regarding accepted recyclable or compostable food packaging. • #7 PLA is Polylactic Acid that is derived from plants instead of oil, like traditional plastics are. Being derived from plants allows the material to breakdown in commercial composting systems.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Occasionally, products other than food packaging such as diapers, toothbrushes, sponges, and starch packing peanuts are labelled “compostable” or “PLA” but have not been tested or approved and do not go in the Food and Yard waste cart.
All coffee bags are garbage. The combination of materials used for the inner and outer liners to keep the coffee fresh; along with the flimsiness of the bag, make this item not recyclable.

Plastic Stand-Up pouches, like Red Mill Oatmeal Pouches, that sit upright on shelves in their plastic packaging are garbage. These stand-up pouches become contaminants in the recycling process and are not recyclable.

disposal_conditions.compost: Pumpkins with a minor amount of paint or coloring on them can go in the food and yard waste container.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Pumpkins completely coated with large amounts of paint, coloring, any glitter, or other material go in the garbage.

disposal_conditions.compost: Food service items that are tested, approved, and labeled "Compostable" with a certification mark (such as “BPI”, TÜV, or CMA) or that are made entirely of wood or paper fiber with no plastic coating, go in the food and yard waste. Compostable food service packaging items may include: • Clamshell-style, hinge-lid paper fiber To-Go food containers • Fold-top To-Go paper boxes • Cups • Plates • Straws and utensils • Portion cups • Paper napkins • Paper towels • Uncoated (not shiny) paper plates and bowls (such as Chinet brand) Learn more about compostable packaging (PDF)
disposal_conditions.garbage: Plastic stickers or labels must be removed from compostable food packaging before going in the compost cart. How to identify plastic stickers and labels: • Harder to tear, elastic, or stretch when attempting to remove • Shiny • Cannot see fibers when cut
All hair, wigs, and pet fur go in the garbage. None of these are compostable. Bag hair, wigs, and pet fur before placing it in the garbage container.
disposal_conditions.donate: Some organizations may accept used wigs. Check online for specific organizations.

disposal_conditions.garbage: All menstrual hygiene products, including those labeled compostable, go in the garbage. Do not flush menstrual hygiene products down a toilet. This can clog pipes and lead to sewer backups. Learn more about What To Flush

items.item_name: Writing, Coloring, and Personal Painting Materials

disposal_conditions.garbage: • All writing and coloring materials such as pens, pencils, markers, and crayons can go in the garbage. • Paint brushes that are dry go in the garbage. Paint brushes are not recyclable nor compostable.
disposal_conditions.donate: If your materials are in good condition consider donating them to a local community organization, school, or thrift store.

disposal_conditions.recycling: What Do I Do With tool to find an electronics recycler near you. Call ahead to make sure the recycler will accept your item.
disposal_conditions.garbage: All headphones and headsets can go in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.donate: If your headphones or headsets still work and are in good condition, consider donating them.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Balloons are garbage. Pop and bag all balloons before placing them in the garbage container.
All sponges and loofahs are garbage, even those labeled compostable. They are not recyclable nor compostable.

items.item_name: Bandages and Other First Aid Materials

disposal_conditions.garbage: Any material used for administering first-aid is garbage. Any material covered in blood or other bodily fluids is garbage. Bag all materials used for first-aid or that are covered in bodily fluids before placing them in the garbage container.

disposal_conditions.compost: Candy without wrappers or plastic can go in the compost. Be sure to remove all wrappers before composting.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Gum and candy with wrappers or plastic go in the garbage.
Water filters go in the garbage. They are made of multiple materials making them hard to recycle. Check with the manufacturer to see if they will take back the filters for recycling.

Empty lighters can go in the garbage. Used and unused matches should be soaked in water for 5 minutes before being disposed of in the garbage. This reduces any chance of the matches igniting. Matches are not compostable or recyclable because of the igniting chemical at the end and their size.
disposal_conditions.hazardous: Lighters with any amount of fuel left must go to a household hazardous waste facility .

disposal_conditions.garbage: Glitter should go in the garbage. Put all glitter in a sealable bag before disposing of it.
Bag earplugs with other waste before disposing of them in the garbage.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: You can also drop off BBQ grills at scrap metal recyclers in your area. Use King County's "What Do I Do With" to find a scrap metal recycler near you. Outdoor grills can be taken to a transfer station and recycled as scrap metal for free. Fuel tanks are not accepted. Small amounts of plastic and other non-metal material are okay.
disposal_conditions.special: Schedule a bulky item pickup for a grill to be collected from your home. Fees apply.

disposal_conditions.garbage: All blades should go in the garbage. Utility blades and double-edge razor blades should be taped over to avoid cutting garbage bags and collectors.
disposal_conditions.dropoff: Take unusable table saws to a transfer station

disposal_conditions.compost: Uncoated, wooden chopsticks can go in the compost.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Plastic and metal chopsticks go in the garbage. They are not recyclable because of their material and size.

Any rope, thread, yarn, or twine with synthetic material, like nylon, can go in the garbage. Typically, if the material is shiny or elastic it contains synthetic fibers. Make sure to place the material in a bag before placing it in the garbage container. This will help the material from becoming tangled in the equipment.
disposal_conditions.donate: Thread and rope can be donated, potentially recycled, or upcycled into new products. • Climbing ropes may be recycled by the rope manufacturer or donated to companies to create new products like pet leashes. Check your local area to see if a business will accept your rope to be recycled or upcycled. • Sewing thread and yarn can be donated to local organizations. Check for local groups in your area. Check out Seattle’s Donate, Resell, & Buy Used page for more information.

items.item_name: Plastic Credit Cards and Gift Cards

disposal_conditions.garbage: Cut plastic cards into several pieces and place them in a garbage bag before placing them in the garbage container.
Cigarettes and Cigarette butts go in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.hazardous: E-cigarettes or other vape devices, and e-liquid waste should not be thrown in the regular trash or flushed down a toilet or sink. Bring vape devices and components to a household hazardous waste facility for safe disposal. No fees. • 10 devices per customer per trip • Marijuana/cannabis e-liquid or vape devices are not accepted at household hazardous waste facilities. Check with the manufacturer or store that you purchased the liquid from for proper disposal.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Makeup can go in the garbage. Bag loose makeup and seal it before placing it in the garbage container. Wipeout and recycle plastic makeup containers that are at least three inches in diameter. Throw wipes in the garbage. Do not rinse makeup down the sink drain.
disposal_conditions.hazardous: Large quantities of makeup related chemicals like acetone (nail polish remover) should be brought to a Household Hazardous Waste Facility.
disposal_conditions.donate: Unexpired or unopened makeup may be donated to certain organizations - particularly women’s or family shelters. Contact a shelter near you to see if they will accept your unused makeup.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Vinyl siding is not recyclable in our curbside system. Please put it in the garbage. The garbage lid needs to be able to close and the bin cannot be heavier than the maximum weight allowed for its size. View rates for the maximum weight allowed for each size of the bin. If your vinyl siding is too large or too heavy, you will either have to schedule a special collection or take the siding to a transfer station for disposal. Note that fees apply for both of these options.
disposal_conditions.dropoff: You can take vinyl siding to a City transfer station for disposal. Fees apply.
disposal_conditions.special: If your vinyl siding is too large or too heavy you can schedule a special collection . Fees apply.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Ribbon is considered garbage. Place ribbon in a bag before disposing of it in a garbage container.
disposal_conditions.donate: Try to reuse ribbons for future events or gifts. Some organizations may accept larger quantities of ribbons, if they are unused, for crafts. Check with your local school or a retirement community near you to see if they are interested in your ribbon.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Floral foam belongs in the garbage. Place the floral foam in a bag before disposing of it in a garbage container. Alternatively, see if the nursery or company that provided the arrangement will take the foam back for reuse. Floral water beads or gel water beads go in the garbage.
Damaged wicker baskets can go in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.donate: Donate any wicker baskets in good condition to local thrift stores.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Boats will need to be dropped off at a transfer station.
disposal_conditions.donate: Donate boats in good condition to nearby boating shops or organizations.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Plastic mesh bags, often containing oranges or potatos cannot be recycled. They go in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.donate: Plastic mesh bags can be reused in variety of ways, simply google "how to reuse plastic mesh produce bag" to get some inspiration!

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Suitcases will need to be dropped off at a transfer station.
disposal_conditions.special: Suitcases will need special item pickup.
disposal_conditions.donate: Donate suitcases or travel bags in good condition to local thrift stores.

disposal_conditions.compost: • If plastic bristles are completely removed, bamboo toothbrushes can go in your compost bin.
disposal_conditions.garbage: • Plastic, non-electric toothbrushes go in the garbage. • Electric toothbrush heads separated from the electric toothbrush go in the garbage.
Absorbent pads are not recyclable and should be put in the garbage.

items.explanation: Absorbent pads always go in the garbage.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Bring water tanks to city transfer stations . Fees apply. Check the rates page .
disposal_conditions.special: Submit a request online or call (206) 684-3000 to schedule a pickup. Fee applies.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Air purifier filters and furnace filters should be individually bagged and put in the garbage.

disposal_conditions.compost: If you are using PLA filament, it is made of biodegradable natural fibers which means it can go in your compost bin.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Filaments made from ABS, polycarbonate, and nylon are not recyclable and should go into your garbage bin.

items.explanation: Always have text here.

disposal_conditions.recycling: Plastic and Metal Ice cube trays go in the recycling if they are clean, dry, and empty.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Silicone ice cube trays go in the garbage.

items.explanation: Empty, Clean and Dry PLASTIC AND METAL ICE CUBE TRAYS go in the recycling. SILICONE ICE CUBE TRAYS go in to garbage.

items.item_name: Unused & Used COVID Rapid Antigen Tests

disposal_conditions.garbage: Unused /expired kits can go into garbage.
disposal_conditions.hazardous: Used kits should be disposed of in a biohazard bag and if possible at a medical facility.

disposal_conditions.compost: Popsicle sticks go into the food waste bin.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Used and unused floss, as well as the dispenser, go into the garbage.
Heavily used zipper bags can go into the garbage.
disposal_conditions.donate: Donate zipper bags in good condition to local thrift stores.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Ladders will need to be dropped at a tranfer station.
disposal_conditions.special: Ladders will need special item pickup.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Baby bottles go into garbage.
disposal_conditions.donate: Donate baby bottles in good condition to local thrift stores.

disposal_conditions.compost: Unpainted and untreated wood can go in the food and yard waste: • Pieces should be less than 4 inches in diameter and under 4 feet long to avoid extra fees • Remove nails and all other non-wood materials • Wood pellets must be broken down to fit in the bin when the lid is closed
disposal_conditions.garbage: Treated or painted wood pellets can go in the garbage. Wood pellets must be broken down to fit in the bin when the lid is closed.
disposal_conditions.dropoff: Large wood pellets can be taken to a local transfer station.
disposal_conditions.donate: Wood pellets in good condition can be reused as storage and decoration.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Nylon and nylon products can go in the garbage.
If not being reused, window screens should be placed in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.donate: Window screens can be reused in a variety of ways! They can be used as an adhesive cleaning tool, flower pot liner, grout mix strainer, and more! Do a quick Google search to get some inspiration. Alternativly, you can resell or give it away using sites like Craigslist, Freecycle, and Buy Nothing.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Glass, wooden, and plastic cutting boards in unusable condition go in the garbage. They cannot be recycled.
disposal_conditions.donate: If cutting boards are in good condition, consider donating them to a local thriftstore where they can be re-used.

disposal_conditions.recycling: Plastic scoopers larger than 3 inches in diameter can go in recycling.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Small plastic scoopers smaller than 3 inches go in the garbage.
Magnets 3 inches or smaller can go in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.dropoff: Take large magnets or large amounts of magnets to transfer station .

disposal_conditions.recycling: Empty, clean, and dry deodorant containers can go in the recycling.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Unused or used deodorant can go in the garbage.

disposal_conditions.recycling: Small, empty clean, and dry hard plastic cat litter boxes can go in recycling.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Soiled carboard cat litter boxes go in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.dropoff: Large cat litter boxes can be dropped off at local transfer stations .
disposal_conditions.special: Submit a request online or call (206) 684-3000 to schedule a pickup. Fee applies.
disposal_conditions.donate: Clean cat litter boxes in good condition can be donated to local thrift stores.

disposal_conditions.recycling: Small plastic crates can go in recycling.
disposal_conditions.dropoff: Large quantities of plastic crates or large crates can be dropped off at a local transfer station .
disposal_conditions.special: Submit a request online or call (206) 684-3000 to schedule a pickup. Fee applies.
disposal_conditions.donate: Plastic crates in good condition can be reused for storage.

disposal_conditions.recycling: Contact the hospital you received the x-ray from to ask if they have recycling services. Or contact a radiology clinic.

disposal_conditions.garbage: If you chose to not reuse them, Silica Gel packets go in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.donate: Silica Gel packets can be reused to keep items dry. Consider keeping them in your gym bag or sneakers. If you use a razor, prevent rust by storing it in a container with the packets. Keep documents/electronics safe and dry with silica packets. Use them for craft projects and dry flowers with them. Get creative!

disposal_conditions.recycling: If the bin you use is a personal bin (i.e., you bought it yourself; it was not provided to you), you may recycle it if it is made of metal or rigid plastic and is empty, clean, and dry.
disposal_conditions.garbage: If the bin you use is a personal bin (i.e., you bought it yourself; it was not provided to you) and it is irreversably contaminated, place it in a large plastic bag if possible and leave it out with your garbage. If you cannot bag it, you can leave it on the curb alongside your bins with a note on it saying it is trash.

disposal_conditions.recycling: If clean, chicken wire and chain link fence are recyclable.
disposal_conditions.garbage: If uncleanable, or treated with chemicals, put wire fencing in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.dropoff: If fencing doesn't fit in your bin, you can bring it to a transfer station.
disposal_conditions.donate: You can reuse chicken wire for a variety of DIY garden/home projects. Alternatively, you can resell or give it away using sites like Craigslist, Freecycle, and Buy Nothing.

disposal_conditions.recycling: The clear plastic containers that berries and tomatos are sold in can be recycled.
disposal_conditions.compost: • Green paper fruit containers can be composted.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Plastic berry baskets (thin green plastic mesh baskets) go in the garbage.

disposal_conditions.recycling: Plastic hand sanitizer bottles that are larger than 3 inches, and are clean, dry and empty can go in the recycling.
disposal_conditions.hazardous: Because hand sanitizer is ignitable, it is considered hazardous waste, and should be disposed of at a household hazardous waste facility. Learn more at www.epa.gov/hw/how-dispose-and-recycle-alcohol-based-hand-sanitizer .

disposal_conditions.compost: Chicken, horse, goat, and rabbit manure is safe to put in your compost bin!

disposal_conditions.donate: • Seattle Recreative is a donation based art supplies store that keeps unwanted art supplies out of landfills. Check out their website for a full list of acceptable items. They have locations in North and South seattle. • You can also donate/sell supplies on Craigslist, Buy Nothing, or your local thrift store.

disposal_conditions.compost: Cut flowers can go in your food and yard waste bin.

disposal_conditions.garbage: If damaged or unusable, bowling balls can go in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.donate: Donate any unwanted bowling balls or equipment to your local thrift store, youth center, or even bowling alley!

disposal_conditions.dropoff: • Did you know you may be eligible for a $500 reward for recycling your old wood-burning stove through the Puget Sound Clean Air Agency’s Wood Stove Recycling Program? Learn more about the program and apply on the Puget Sound Clean Air Agency website . Note, the program has specific requirements and you must be able to (or make arrangements to) uninstall and drop off the stove at approved locations; the program does not offer stove removal or delivery services. • If you do not meet the requirements for this buy-back program, you can contact area scrap metal recyclers to see if they accept old wood stoves and recycle it on our own.

disposal_conditions.garbage: • Solid fertilizers with no pesticides can go in the garbage. • Liquid fertilizers with no pesticides can be solidified using dirt, kitty litter, paper towels, or other absorbant materials and put in the garbage.
disposal_conditions.hazardous: Bring fertilizers with pesticides or herbicides, often called weed and feed, to a hazardous waste collection facility . All containers must be 5 gallons or less.
disposal_conditions.dropoff: If you have organic fertilizer, check with your local gardening group or contact the Garden Hotline • to see if you can donate it.
